commonProblems.h2: Audi Q2 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Audi Q2 1.4 TFSI COD Cylinder Deactivation Failure
problems.h4: Audi Q2 1.4 TFSI COD Cylinder Deactivation Failure - Why CZEA Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Audi Q2 1.4 TFSI, 1.4 TFSI COD, SE, Sport, S line (2016-2020, CZEA)
problems.rootCause: The Cylinder on Demand (COD) system uses solenoid-operated valve lifters to deactivate cylinders under light load. These solenoids fail mechanically or electrically, causing misfires, irregular running, and eventually bent valves or piston damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed COD solenoids and reset adaptions
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught immediately; prevents further damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace affected valves and solenoids, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: 12-24 months; high risk of repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete CZEA engine with upgraded COD components, bench tested, 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Q2 shows early COD warning signs (misfire codes, uneven running, engine light), act immediately. Solenoid replacement is relatively affordable and can prevent catastrophic failure. However, if the engine has already suffered valve damage or you're beyond 60,000 miles, we recommend full replacement with a rebuilt unit that includes upgraded solenoids - the extra cost justifies the long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CZEA 1.4 TFSI engine prices for your Audi Q2

problems.group: Audi Q2 1.4 TFSI Excessive Oil Consumption
problems.h4: Audi Q2 1.4 TFSI Excessive Oil Consumption - Piston Ring & Oil Scraper Failure
problems.affectedModels: Audi Q2 1.4 TFSI, 1.4 TFSI COD (2016-2020, CZEA engine)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup and gumming of low-tension piston oil control rings leads to ring sticking. Oil passes into combustion chambers, causing high consumption (1L per 600-1,000 miles) and eventual bore glazing or ringland fracture.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical decarbonisation, piston soak, oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary (3-12 months); may delay replacement only

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace piston rings and seals, hone bores
Complete unit with redesigned piston rings, fully balanced, 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: Once a CZEA engine is consuming more than 0.5L per 1,000 miles, engine flushes are a temporary delay at best. Piston ring replacement is expensive for what remains a partially worn engine. On a typical Q2 worth £8,000-£14,000, full replacement with a rebuilt unit using upgraded piston rings is the most cost-effective long-term decision. The cost difference between intermediate and full replacement is often only £300-£600 - choose replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt CZEA engine with upgraded piston rings

problems.group: Audi Q2 2.0 TDI DPF & EGR System Failure
problems.h4: Audi Q2 2.0 TDI DPF & EGR System Failure - Regeneration & Blockage Problems
problems.affectedModels: Audi Q2 30 TDI, 35 TDI, S line, Black Edition (2017-2024, DFGA, DJGA, DDYA engines)
problems.rootCause: The 2.0 TDI requires regular long runs for passive DPF regeneration. Urban driving leads to incomplete regens, soot buildup, and ultimately DPF blockage. EGR valves clog simultaneously from recirculated soot, creating compounding restriction.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic forced regen, chemical DPF clean, EGR clean
Replace DPF filter and EGR valve, reset adaptions
Complete engine with new DPF-ready injectors, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles; only if engine has internal damage

problems.recommendation: If your Q2's DPF light is on but regeneration completes, a forced regen and chemical clean is worth trying first. However, if the filter is fully blocked (ash load over 80%) or the EGR has failed, replacement of both components costs £700-£1,000 at a specialist. Only opt for full engine replacement if the DPF failure has caused turbo seal damage or injector fouling - a specialist inspection is essential before deciding.
problems.cta: Compare 2.0 TDI rebuilt engine quotes for your Audi Q2

problems.h4: Audi Q2 1.6 TDI Injector Failure - Common Rail Injector Collapse
problems.affectedModels: Audi Q2 30 TDI (2016-2019, CYKB 1.6 TDI engine)
problems.rootCause: Bosch piezoelectric injectors in the 1.6 TDI suffer internal needle or solenoid wear. Diesel leaks past seals into the cylinder, causing hydraulic lock at worst, or simply misfires, rough idle, and excessive diesel knock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors, fuel rail, and seals
Complete engine with new injectors installed
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles; necessary if bore damage occurred

problems.recommendation: Injector failure on a 1.6 TDI often starts with one cylinder, but the remaining three are typically near the end of their service life. We recommend replacing all four injectors in one go at a specialist cost of £900-£1,300 - this is cheaper than repeated single failures. Only consider full engine replacement if a failed injector has hydrolocked the engine and bent a connecting rod, which a compression test will confirm.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CYKB 1.6 TDI rebuilt engines

problems.group: Audi Q2 1.0 TFSI Engine Vibration & Water Pump Failure
problems.h4: Audi Q2 1.0 TFSI Engine Vibration & Water Pump Failure
problems.affectedModels: Audi Q2 1.0 TFSI, SE, Sport, S line (2017-2020, DADA engine)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000 - 60,000 miles (water pump); vibration present from 20,000 miles
problems.rootCause: Three-cylinder 1.0 TFSI engines have inherent imbalance that wears engine mounts prematurely. Water pumps on this EA211 family develop leaks from the plastic housing or internal seal failure, leading to coolant loss and potential overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete DADA unit with upgraded mounts and pump

problems.recommendation: The 1.0 TFSI is generally robust, but water pump failure is extremely common - replace it immediately when coolant drops to avoid overheating damage. Engine vibration is annoying but not catastrophic; new mounts restore smooth running. Full engine replacement is rarely needed for this unit unless a neglected coolant leak caused head gasket failure. A specialist can replace the pump and belt for around £300-£500.
problems.cta: Compare DADA 1.0 TFSI rebuilt engine prices

problems.group: Audi Q2 2.0 TFSI Timing Chain Stretch
problems.h4: Audi Q2 2.0 TFSI Timing Chain Stretch - Quattro Petrol Engine Weak Point
problems.affectedModels: Audi Q2 2.0 TFSI, 2.0 TFSI quattro, S line (2017-2020, CZPB engine)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner design on the CZPB allows gradual chain stretch over time. If not addressed, chain slack causes timing drift, rattling on startup, and eventual valve-to-piston contact with catastrophic engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioner, and sprockets
Complete CZPB unit with upgraded timing components

problems.recommendation: The 2.0 TFSI engine is expensive to replace, so preventative timing chain replacement at 70,000-80,000 miles is excellent value at a specialist cost of £900-£1,400. Listen for chain rattle on cold starts - that's your warning sign. If the chain has already jumped timing or snapped, full replacement is the only option. Given the Q2 2.0 TFSI's relatively low numbers, sourcing a quality rebuilt unit from a specialist is essential - dealer prices are prohibitive.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CZPB 2.0 TFSI rebuilt engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Audi Q2
finalCta.paragraph: The 1.4 TFSI COD's cylinder deactivation and oil consumption issues, alongside diesel DPF and injector failures, are the main reasons Q2 owners face major repair decisions. But replacement doesn't mean dealer prices or the scrap yard. EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Audi Q2 specialists - every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, full bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Audi Q2 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Audi Q2 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Audi Q2 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Audi Q2 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years, power outputs, and common failure notes for diesel and petrol options.

items.title: Audi Q2 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 TDI was the entry-level diesel for the early pre-facelift Q2 (2016-2019), badged as 30 TDI. It offered respectable economy for urban and mixed driving but gained a reputation for injector failure as mileages climbed beyond 60,000 miles. This unit was phased out after the 2020 facelift in favour of updated 2.0 TDI variants.
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi Q2 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Audi Q2 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the Q2 diesel range, the 2.0 TDI appeared in both 116hp (30 TDI) and 150hp (35 TDI) tunes across pre-facelift and facelift generations. Quattro-equipped variants used a different engine code with revised mounting points. The 2.0 TDI remained available until the Q2's discontinuation, favoured by higher-mileage drivers for its motorway refinement and torque.
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi Q2 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Audi Q2 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The smallest petrol option in the Q2 range, the 1.0 TFSI three-cylinder was aimed at urban drivers and ULEZ-conscious buyers. Fitted only to pre-facelift models (2017-2020), it offered surprising punch for its size but suffered from inherent vibration that wore engine mounts prematurely. Water pump failure is the most common replacement driver.
items.cta: Compare Audi Q2 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi Q2 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most notorious engine in the Q2 line-up, the 1.4 TFSI with Cylinder on Demand (COD) technology was the pre-facelift performance petrol choice from 2016 to 2020. COD promised efficiency but delivered solenoid failures and oil consumption issues that dominate replacement search volume. Despite its reputation, when running correctly it offered a superb blend of 150hp power and reasonable economy.
commonFailurePoints: excessive oil consumption from stuck piston rings
items.cta: Compare Audi Q2 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi Q2 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The facelift replacement for the troubled 1.4 TFSI, the 1.5 TFSI arrived in 2020 with mild hybrid (MHEV) technology on select variants. Badged as 35 TFSI, it offered similar power with revised COD timing and a belt alternator starter. While more reliable than its predecessor, early MHEV units have shown hesitation and BAS module failures that require specialist attention.
commonFailurePoints: belt alternator starter (BAS) failure on MHEV units
items.cta: Compare Audi Q2 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi Q2 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The range-topping petrol unit, the 2.0 TFSI was exclusively available with quattro all-wheel drive on pre-facelift Q2s (2017-2020). Badged simply as 2.0 TFSI quattro, it delivered 190hp and transformed the Q2 into a genuine warm SUV. Low production volumes mean replacement engines are scarce and command a premium over the rest of the range.
items.cta: Compare Audi Q2 2.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Audi Q2 Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Audi Q2 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the 2016 1.4 TFSI to the facelift 35 TFSI MHEV.

items.title: Audi Q2 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel powered the majority of UK Q2 sales, from the 1.6 TDI entry point to the 2.0 TDI quattro, with replacement demand spiking around 60,000-80,000 miles as DPF and injector issues emerge.
items.descriptor: Diesel powered the majority of UK Q2 sales, from the 1.6 TDI entry point to the 2.0 TDI quattro, with replacement demand spiking around 60,000-80,000 miles as DPF and injector issues emerge.
families: CYKB - 1.6 TDI, entry-level 30 TDI, injector failure common
DFGA - 2.0 TDI 116hp, most common 30 TDI pre-facelift unit
DJGA - 2.0 TDI 150hp, facelift 35 TDI higher-output version
DDYA - 2.0 TDI 150hp quattro, quattro-specific mounting points
foundIn: Audi Q2 pre-facelift (2016-2020) - CYKB 1.6 TDI, DFGA 2.0 TDI, DDYA 2.0 TDI quattro
Audi Q2 facelift (2020-2024) - DJGA 2.0 TDI (35 TDI)
knownFor: Strong motorway torque and 50+ mpg real-world economy
DPF pressure sensor failures on short-journey cars (50,000-90,000 miles)
Injector seal and EGR cooler leaks on 2.0 TDI units
1.6 TDI injector collapse - £1,800 specialist replacement cost
typicalModels: S line / Black Edition / Vorsprung diesel variants

items.title: Audi Q2 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Q2s range from the economical 1.0 TFSI three-cylinder to the notorious 1.4 TFSI COD and the facelift 1.5 TFSI mild hybrid, with the 1.4 TFSI dominating replacement searches due to cylinder deactivation failures.
items.descriptor: Petrol Q2s range from the economical 1.0 TFSI three-cylinder to the notorious 1.4 TFSI COD and the facelift 1.5 TFSI mild hybrid, with the 1.4 TFSI dominating replacement searches due to cylinder deactivation failures.
families: DADA - 1.0 TFSI three-cylinder, entry-level petrol, water pump weakness
CZEA - 1.4 TFSI COD, most problematic Q2 engine, solenoid and oil consumption failures
DARB - 1.5 TFSI MHEV, facelift replacement for CZEA, BAS module issues
CZPB - 2.0 TFSI, quattro-only performance variant, timing chain stretch risk
foundIn: Audi Q2 pre-facelift (2016-2020) - DADA 1.0 TFSI, CZEA 1.4 TFSI COD, CZPB 2.0 TFSI
Audi Q2 facelift (2020-2024) - DARB 1.5 TFSI MHEV (35 TFSI)
knownFor: 1.4 TFSI COD cylinder deactivation solenoid failure (30,000-60,000 miles)
Excessive oil consumption from stuck piston rings on CZEA units
1.0 TFSI engine mount vibration and water pump leaks
2.0 TFSI timing chain stretch - preventative replacement advised at 70k miles
Facelift 1.5 TFSI belt alternator starter failures
typicalModels: S line / Black Edition / Vorsprung petrol variants

items.title: Audi Q2 Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: The facelift Q2 introduced 48V mild hybrid technology on 35 TFSI petrol models from 2020, using a belt alternator starter instead of a traditional starter motor.
items.descriptor: The facelift Q2 introduced 48V mild hybrid technology on 35 TFSI petrol models from 2020, using a belt alternator starter instead of a traditional starter motor.
families: DARB - 1.5 TFSI with 48V belt alternator starter (BAS)
48V starter-generator recovers braking energy to charge a lithium-ion battery
Provides torque assistance under acceleration and enables coasting
Engine replacement requires BAS inspection and battery health check
foundIn: Audi Q2 35 TFSI, 35 TFSI Sport, 35 TFSI S line, 35 TFSI Black Edition, 35 TFSI Vorsprung (2020-2024)
importantNotes: No plug-in hybrid or full electric Q2 was sold in the UK
MHEV engine replacement is more complex than standard petrol units
BAS module failure is a known weakness on early facelift cars
Availability of rebuilt MHEV units is more limited than non-hybrid engines

engineYears.h2: Audi Q2 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section maps every major engine change across the Audi Q2 production timeline from 2016 to 2024, helping owners identify their engine code and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Audi Q2 launched in late 2016 on the GA platform, bringing the controversial 1.4 TFSI COD and the 1.6/2.0 TDI range to UK roads.
keyChanges: Launch of the first-generation Q2 (GA platform, Ingolstadt production)
Introduction of 1.4 TFSI COD with cylinder deactivation technology
1.6 TDI and 2.0 TDI diesel options available from launch
2.0 TFSI quattro added to range in late 2017
Seven-speed S tronic DCT standard on most variants
knownFor: 1.4 TFSI COD cylinder deactivation failures from 30,000 miles
CZEA oil consumption from stuck piston rings (40,000-70,000 miles)
1.6 TDI injector failures on CYKB units
2.0 TDI DFGA DPF pressure sensor issues
S tronic transmission hesitation at low speeds
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2018 Audi Q2 replacement engines
years.ticker: CZEA engine failure 1.4 TFSI • Audi Q2 1.4 TFSI COD problems • 1.6 TDI injector failure Q2 • DFGA DPF issues

years.description: The 1.5 TFSI arrived in late 2018, replacing the problematic 1.4 TFSI COD in the 35 TFSI, while the SQ2 launched with 300hp.
keyChanges: 1.4 TFSI COD (CZEA) phased out, replaced by 1.5 TFSI (DARB)
Introduction of DARB with mild hybrid preparation (48V-ready architecture)
SQ2 launched with 2.0 TFSI (300hp) - DJH/DNW engines
1.0 TFSI DADA added as entry-level petrol
knownFor: 1.5 TFSI DARB - more reliable than CZEA but BAS issues on later MHEV cars
1.0 TFSI water pump failures (40,000-60,000 miles)
SQ2 timing chain stretch risk on high-mileage cars
DJGA 2.0 TDI EGR and injector seal failures
S tronic reliability improved on facelift preparation cars
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2020 Audi Q2 replacement engines
years.ticker: DARB 1.5 TFSI engine hesitation • SQ2 engine replacement cost • 1.0 TFSI water pump failure Q2 • 2.0 TDI DJGA EGR failure

years.description: The 2020 facelift brought revised styling, new MHEV technology on the 35 TFSI, and the retirement of the 1.4 TFSI and 1.6 TDI.
keyChanges: Facelift exterior (new grille, lights, bumpers) from late 2020
35 TFSI now full MHEV with 48V belt alternator starter (BAS)
1.6 TDI (CYKB) discontinued - 30 TDI now 2.0L DFGA only
40 TFSI (2.0L petrol) discontinued in UK from 2022
Diesel range simplified to 2.0 TDI only (116hp and 150hp)
knownFor: 1.5 TFSI MHEV BAS unit failures on early facelift cars
48V battery degradation after 4-5 years
Engine stalling on coasting restart (BAS or software related)
2.0 TDI DJGA remains most reliable diesel option
DPF issues still present for urban-only driven cars
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2024 Audi Q2 replacement engines
years.ticker: 35 TFSI MHEV BAS failure • DARB 1.5 TFSI stalling • 2.0 TDI DJGA replacement cost • 48V battery replacement Q2

faq.h2: Audi Q2 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the Audi Q2 1.4 TFSI COD engine reliable?
items.answer: No, the 1.4 TFSI COD (CZEA) is widely considered the least reliable Q2 engine, with cylinder deactivation failures and oil consumption common from 30,000 miles.
keyPoints: COD solenoid failure causes rough running and misfires
Excessive oil consumption from stuck piston rings after 40,000 miles
Timing chain rattle on startup is a known warning sign
Replacement often more cost-effective than repair on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare rebuilt CZEA engine prices for your Audi Q2

items.question: How much does an Audi Q2 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Audi Q2 engine supply typically costs £1,400-£3,200, with fitting adding £450-£800. The 1.4 TFSI COD is most expensive due to demand.
keyPoints: 1.0 TFSI rebuilt: £1,400-£1,900 supply only
1.4 TFSI COD rebuilt: £1,800-£2,400 supply only
2.0 TDI rebuilt: £1,600-£2,300 supply only
Supply & fit complete: £2,000-£4,000 depending on engine
Used engines cheaper: £1,200-£1,900 with shorter warranty
items.cta: Get instant Audi Q2 engine quotes for your specific engine code

items.question: What are the most common Audi Q2 engine problems?
items.answer: The 1.4 TFSI COD cylinder deactivation failure dominates complaints, followed by 2.0 TDI DPF issues, 1.6 TDI injector failure, and 1.0 TFSI water pump leaks.
keyPoints: CZEA 1.4 TFSI: COD solenoid failure (30,000-60,000 miles)
2.0 TDI: DPF pressure sensor failure and EGR cooler leaks
1.6 TDI: Injector collapse (£1,500-£2,000 replacement)
1.0 TFSI: Water pump housing leak (40,000-60,000 miles)
S tronic transmission judder reported from 20,000-40,000 km
items.cta: Find a specialist for Audi Q2 engine diagnostics and quotes

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on an Audi Q2?
items.answer: Yes, if the Q2 is worth over £6,000-£8,000 and the body/gearbox are sound. A rebuilt engine with warranty costs £1,800-£3,200 - far less than a replacement car.
keyPoints: Typical Q2 valuation around £10,600 makes replacement viable
Rebuilt engine includes upgraded components fixing original failure points
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides peace of mind
Supply & fit complete still cheaper than main dealer new car finance
Not worth it for high-mileage (120k+) or heavily damaged examples
items.cta: Compare Audi Q2 rebuilt engine prices vs replacement car cost

items.question: How long does an Audi Q2 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply only takes 3-5 working days. Supply & fit typically takes 5-10 working days total, depending on garage availability and engine type.
keyPoints: Used engines often next-day delivery
Rebuilt/reconditioned engines: 10-15 working days lead time
Fitting normally 1-3 days once engine arrives
Complex engines (2.0 TFSI quattro) may take longer
Mobile fitters available in some areas for faster turnaround
items.cta: Get estimated delivery times for your Audi Q2 engine code

items.question: Which Audi Q2 engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The 2.0 TDI DJGA (150hp) from facelift models is the most robust, followed by the 1.5 TFSI DARB. Avoid the 1.4 TFSI COD and early 1.6 TDI for long-term ownership.
keyPoints: 2.0 TDI DJGA: Fewer DPF issues than earlier DFGA units
1.5 TFSI DARB: More reliable than CZEA but watch for BAS failures
1.0 TFSI: Simple three-cylinder but water pump weak point
1.4 TFSI COD: Avoid - known COD and oil consumption issues
1.6 TDI CYKB: Injector failure common from 60,000 miles
items.cta: Compare prices on the most reliable Audi Q2 rebuilt engines

items.question: What is the difference between Audi Q2 petrol and diesel engines for replacement?
items.answer: Petrol engines (especially 1.4 TFSI) fail earlier with COD/oil issues. Diesels last longer but suffer DPF, EGR and injector problems - choose based on your driving pattern.
keyPoints: Petrol failure: Typically 30,000-70,000 miles (COD or oil consumption)
Diesel failure: Typically 60,000-90,000 miles (DPF or injectors)
Petrol rebuilt: £1,400-£3,200 supply only
Diesel rebuilt: £1,500-£2,300 supply only
Diesels need regular long runs for DPF regeneration
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi Q2 petrol or diesel engine replacement

items.question: Does the Audi Q2 have timing chain or belt issues?
items.answer: Most Q2 engines use timing belts (1.0/1.4/1.5 TFSI, 1.6 TDI). The 2.0 TDI and 2.0 TFSI have chains - the 2.0 TFSI CZPB is known for chain stretch after 70,000 miles.
keyPoints: 1.0/1.4/1.5 TFSI: Belt - replace every 5 years or 75,000 miles
1.6/2.0 TDI: Belt - replace every 5 years or 75,000 miles
2.0 TFSI CZPB: Chain - stretch risk from 70,000 miles
Chain replacement on 2.0 TFSI: Specialist £900-£1,400
Belt failure destroys engine - stick to replacement intervals
items.cta: Check timing chain/belt replacement costs for your Audi Q2

items.question: What are Audi Q2 S tronic transmission problems and do they affect the engine?
items.answer: S tronic dual-clutch judder and hesitation affects Q2s from 20,000-40,000 km. Not engine-related but often leads owners to consider full powertrain replacement.
keyPoints: Low-speed jerking and delayed shifts common
Requires software updates or clutch replacement
Independent specialist: £900-£2,500 for gearbox repair
Dealer prices significantly higher for DSG work
Not a reason to replace the engine - transmission separate issue
items.cta: Get quotes for complete Audi Q2 engine and gearbox replacement

items.question: Do you supply Audi Q2 engines with fitting included?
items.answer: Yes, EnginesMarket connects you with UK garages and mobile fitters offering supply & fit - typically £450-£800 added to engine price for full installation.
keyPoints: One invoice, one point of contact
Old engine collected and disposed of
Single warranty covering parts and labour (12 months)
Mobile fitter option available in many UK areas
Fitting quotes provided before any work starts
items.cta: Find Audi Q2 engine supply and fit deals near you
